name: MITRE.LIN.T1053.RULE Scheduled Task/Job
notes:
The following auditd rules should be enabled:

-w /etc/crontab -k siem-scheduling
-w /etc/cron.d/ -k siem-scheduling
-w /var/spool/cron/ -k siem-scheduling
-w /etc/cron.allow -p wa -k siem-scheduling
-w /etc/cron.deny -p wa -k siem-scheduling
-w /etc/cron.d/ -p wa -k siem-scheduling
-w /etc/cron.daily/ -p wa -k siem-scheduling
-w /etc/cron.hourly/ -p wa -k siem-scheduling
-w /etc/cron.monthly/ -p wa -k siem-scheduling
-w /etc/cron.weekly/ -p wa -k siem-scheduling
-w /etc/crontab -p wa -k siem-scheduling
-w /var/spool/cron/crontabs/ -k siem-scheduling
-w /etc/inittab -p wa -k siem-scheduling
-w /etc/init.d/ -p wa -k siem-scheduling
-w /etc/init/ -p wa -k siem-scheduling
-w /etc/anacrontab -p wa -k siem-scheduling

-w /etc/at.allow -p wa -k siem-scheduling
-w /etc/at.deny/ -p wa -k siem-scheduling
-w /var/spool/at/ -p wa -k siem-scheduling

Get more Linux MITRE rules: https://www.scnsoft.com/services/security/siem/linux-mitre-attack-rules
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Linux OS
when the event matches Linux Auditd Key (custom) is any of siem-scheduling
when any of Linux UID (custom) are contained in any of MITRE: Linux Users - AlphaNumeric (Ignore Case)
TD_list: APPLY MITRE.LIN.T1053.RULE Scheduled Task/Job on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Linux OS
AND when the event matches Linux Auditd Key (custom) is any of siem-scheduling
AND NOT when any of Linux UID (custom) are contained in any of MITRE: Linux Users - AlphaNumeric (Ignore Case)
RED_list:
Event description: Suspicious actions with job scheduling commands. 
See more on: https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1053
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
Include detected events by "Source IP" from this point forward, in the offense, for 0 second(s)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
